verses.text: Job feels his breath failing, his days spent, the grave ready, and mockers all around him. He stands at death's door amid scorn. To face the end surrounded by contempt is among the heaviest of trials.

Job feels his breath failing, his days spent, the grave ready, and mockers all around him. He stands at death's door amid scorn. To face the end surrounded by contempt is among the heaviest of trials.

He begs God to be his surety, for no one else will, and laments friends whose hearts God has hidden from understanding. He casts himself on God as his only pledge. When all human backing fails, the believer's security must be God Himself.

He begs God to be his surety, for no one else will, and laments friends whose hearts God has hidden from understanding. He casts himself on God as his only pledge. When all human backing fails, the believer's security must be God Himself.

He begs God to be his surety, for no one else will, and laments friends whose hearts God has hidden from understanding. He casts himself on God as his only pledge. When all human backing fails, the believer's security must be God Himself.

Made a byword and spat upon, his eye dim with grief, Job yet declares the righteous 'shall hold on his way' and grow stronger. Even in the dust, his confidence in final perseverance flickers. Faith insists that the upright will not, in the end, be put to shame.

He finds not one wise man among his friends, who 'change the night into day,' calling darkness light. Their comfort only confuses. False counsel inverts reality, telling the suffering that their midnight is morning.

He finds not one wise man among his friends, who 'change the night into day,' calling darkness light. Their comfort only confuses. False counsel inverts reality, telling the suffering that their midnight is morning.

If the grave is to be his home and corruption his family, where then is his hope? It seems to descend with him to the bars of the pit. Despair would bury hope in the tomb. Yet the very question prepares his heart for the answer faith will soon seize.
